topic: Aside browser developer surface for an mcp-tooling skill mode: the Aside CLI command surface and the Aside MCP server (tools, auth, transport, install, session/daemon model) per https://docs.aside.com/help/developers#use-mcp — AI-browser-automation workflows (navigate, DOM inspection, screenshots, console/network capture), contrast with Chrome DevTools bdg patterns, everything needed to author the mcp-aside-devtools packet (CLI-primary + Code Mode MCP fallback) and register an aside UTCP manual in .utcp_config.json

focus: Official Aside CLI and MCP surface
noveltyJustification: Eight independently cited findings establish the real CLI, stdio MCP transport, single repl tool, browser-profile prerequisite, and account/permission boundary; only browser-bound execution remains untested.
keyQuestions: What is the complete stable Aside CLI command and option surface for browser automation?
What exact MCP tools and schemas does aside mcp expose, and what is the session/daemon lifecycle?
Which auth, privacy, permission, and unattended-automation boundaries must mcp-aside-devtools document?
answeredQuestions: Aside ships a standalone CLI with account, exec, repl, and mcp subcommands.
aside mcp is a local stdio MCP server whose live inventory contains one repl tool.
A fresh aside mcp process requires an Aside browser-profile/task binding before browser operations.
ruledOut: Separate Aside MCP tools named navigate, dom, screenshot, console, or network
Documented remote HTTP/SSE MCP endpoint or static bearer-token configuration
Treating a fresh aside mcp process as sufficient to control any browser

focus: Aside lifecycle, accounts, and unattended boundaries
noveltyJustification: Lifecycle review adds the task-session versus REPL distinction, account-selection boundary, observable stdio/idle lifecycle, documented permission and human-approval gates, update behavior, and a supportability boundary around undocumented daemon internals; residual overlap is expected because the max-iteration policy requires breadth before synthesis.
keyQuestions: What is the complete stable Aside CLI command and option surface for browser automation?
What exact MCP tools and schemas does aside mcp expose, and what is the session/daemon lifecycle?
Which auth, privacy, permission, and unattended-automation boundaries must mcp-aside-devtools document?
answeredQuestions: aside --session continues an agent task and is distinct from MCP browser-profile binding.
Account selection is exposed on aside and aside exec, not documented on aside mcp.
The stdio MCP process can persist while remaining unbound to a browser profile.
Unattended execution is conditional on permissions, profile state, and human-verification gates.
ruledOut: Treating --session as an MCP browser-target selector
Treating a signed-in account as sufficient for unattended execution
Depending on undocumented daemon subcommands or private timeout flags

graphEvents.label: Task continuation differs from MCP REPL binding
Permissions and human verification remain gates

blockers: CLI/MCP lifecycle is bounded but bdg mapping and UTCP packet shape remain unreviewed.
Browser-bound navigation, DOM, screenshot, and console/network capability remains untested without an authorized profile.

focus: Aside packet design, bdg contrast, and UTCP registration
noveltyJustification: The final breadth pass turns the verified surface into a packet design: local stdio UTCP object, runtime discovery and expected Code Mode naming, CLI-primary versus fallback routing, a workflow matrix against bdg, and explicit console/network and bound-page evidence gaps.
keyQuestions: How do Aside's CLI-primary and Code Mode MCP fallback map to the bdg browser-debugging workflow and UTCP registration?
Which auth, privacy, permission, and unattended-automation boundaries must mcp-aside-devtools document?
answeredQuestions: The recommended UTCP manual is a local stdio aside mcp server with no remote auth fields.
The expected Code Mode callable follows aside.aside_repl after runtime discovery.
Aside navigation, DOM inspection, and screenshots map through REPL/Playwright code; console and network are capability probes, not verified dedicated tools.
Aside and bdg have different session, concurrency, and security models.
ruledOut: Treating Aside as a CDP-domain-complete replacement for bdg
Claiming stable aside_console, aside_network, or aside_har MCP tools
Registering Aside as remote HTTP/SSE MCP or adding bearer/OAuth configuration
Treating aside.aside_repl as verified without Code Mode discovery

graphEvents.label: UTCP, Code Mode, and bdg repository conventions

blockers: Bound-page Aside output and console/network listener behavior remain validation gaps.
The derived Code Mode callable requires post-registration discovery.
